result:
I’d make “basic needs” a guaranteed global baseline—food, clean water, shelter, essential healthcare (including mental health), and primary education—funded and delivered in ways that are locally controlled and resilient.

That single shift reduces suffering immediately, expands real freedom (people can say no to exploitation), stabilizes societies, and makes progress on almost everything else—crime, conflict, inequality, public health, and even climate adaptation—far more achievable.

content.text:

That single shift reduces suffering immediately, expands real freedom (people can say no to exploitation), stabilizes societies, and makes progress on almost everything else—crime, conflict, inequality, public health, and even climate adaptation—far more achievable.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
